title: Peace League Africa Awards Gala 2026
tagline: Celebrating excellence in peace-building across Africa — 62 categories, one unforgettable ceremony
description: The Peace League Africa Awards Gala honours exceptional individuals and organizations working to build peace across the continent. With 62 award categories spanning peace advocacy, humanitarian service, youth leadership, and community development, this landmark ceremony brings together peacebuilders from 12 African countries at the historic August 7th Memorial Park in Nairobi.
address: Haile Selassie Avenue (next to Cooperative Bank), Nairobi

speakers.name: H.E. Fatuma Mohamed
speakers.role: Cabinet Secretary, Ministry of Interior & Coordination — Kenya

speakers.name: Amb. Olusegun Akinlade
speakers.role: African Union Special Envoy for Conflict Resolution

highlights: 62 award categories recognizing peace champions across Africa
Keynote address by H.E. Cabinet Secretary Fatuma Mohamed

schedule.title: Opening Ceremony — National Anthem & Welcome Address
Award Session 1: Peace Advocacy & Humanitarian Service
Award Session 2: Youth Leadership & Community Development
Award Session 3: Special Recognitions & Lifetime Achievement
Closing Remarks — A Call to Action for Peace

content:
The Peace League Africa Awards Gala 2026 is the continent's premier celebration of peacebuilding excellence. Taking place at the historic August 7th Memorial Park in Nairobi — a site dedicated to remembrance, resilience, and the triumph of peace over violence — this landmark ceremony honours 62 categories of peace champions from across 12 African countries.

From peace ambassadors fighting radicalism and terrorism, to youth leaders building bridges in divided communities, to humanitarians providing life-saving aid in conflict zones — every nominee represents the very best of Africa's peacebuilding spirit. The awards ceremony is the culmination of months of public nominations, community vetting, and transparent voting.

Why August 7th Memorial Park?

The choice of venue carries profound symbolic weight. The August 7th Memorial Park stands on the site of the 1998 US Embassy bombing, a tragedy that claimed 218 lives and reshaped Kenya's relationship with peace and security. By hosting our awards ceremony here, we honour the memory of those lost while celebrating the peacebuilders who — every day — choose hope over hate, dialogue over division, and reconciliation over revenge.

What to Expect

Guests will enjoy a full-day programme featuring a keynote address by H.E. Cabinet Secretary Fatuma Mohamed, three award sessions spanning peace advocacy, youth leadership, community development, and lifetime achievement recognitions, cultural performances from five African nations, and a networking reception bringing together over 500 peace leaders, policymakers, civil society organizations, and grassroots changemakers.

Dress Code

African formal / Business attire. Traditional regalia encouraged and celebrated.

Tickets

Premium Seats: KES 6,500 (front rows, priority seating, programme booklet, certificate of attendance)
Standard Seats: KES 3,500 (main seating area, programme booklet)
Early Bird Standard: KES 2,500 (limited availability)

Group discounts available for organizations sending 10+ delegates. Contact us at awards@peaceleagueafrica.org for group bookings.

All proceeds support Peace League Africa's peacebuilding programs across the continent.

title: HWPL Interfaith Dialogue: Addressing Hate Speech Through Scriptures
tagline: Addressing Hate Speech and Promoting Peaceful Communication Through Scriptures
description: Religious Leaders and Scholars from different faiths gather to promote peace, understanding, respect, and harmony through interfaith dialogue.

speakers.name: Dr. Hassan Kinyua Omari
speakers.role: Chairperson, Peace League Africa · Islamic Law Scholar · Certified Mediator

speakers.name: Archbishop David Mugambi
speakers.role: Anglican Church of Kenya — Nairobi Diocese

speakers.name: Elder Muthoni wa Njuguna
speakers.role: Council of Traditional Elders — Central Kenya

speakers.name: Swami Prakashananda
speakers.role: Hindu Council of Africa — Nairobi Temple

highlights: Opening prayer circle with all faiths
Panel: Addressing hate speech through scripture
Breakout dialogue groups by faith tradition
content:
On behalf of HWPL Global 12, we kindly invite you to the HWPL Interfaith Dialogue addressing hate speech and promoting peaceful communication through scriptures. Religious Leaders and Scholars from different faiths will gather to promote peace, understanding, respect, and harmony.

The dialogue will explore how scriptures from various faith traditions provide guidance on addressing hate speech and fostering peaceful communication. Participants will have the opportunity to engage in open dialogue, share perspectives, and contribute to a joint declaration for peaceful communication.

We encourage you to invite at least two Religious Leaders or Scholars to accompany you. Light refreshments will be provided. All are welcome to attend this important conversation for peace.

title: Peace Education Summit 2026
tagline: Shaping the future of peace curriculum across East Africa
description: Bringing together educators, community leaders, and policymakers to advance peace curriculum across East African schools.
highlights: Keynote address by Minister of Education
Workshop: Integrating peace into STEM curriculum

schedule.title: Keynote: The State of Peace Education

content:
The Peace Education Summit is the premier gathering for educators, policymakers, and community leaders committed to building peace through education. Now in its fourth year, the summit brings together over 300 participants from across East Africa to share best practices, develop new curriculum frameworks, and forge partnerships that bring peace education to every classroom.

This year's theme is Curriculum as a Bridge — exploring how peace education can be integrated not as a standalone subject, but woven into existing curricula across STEM, humanities, and the arts. Research shows that students who receive peace education are 67% less likely to engage in violent conflict and 43% more likely to complete their schooling.

Join us in Nairobi for a day of inspiration, collaboration, and tangible action. Whether you're a classroom teacher, a ministry official, or a community organizer, you'll leave with practical tools and a network of committed peacebuilders.

title: Youth Leadership Workshop
tagline: Empowering the next generation of peace leaders
description: A three-day intensive workshop empowering young leaders with conflict resolution, public speaking, and civic engagement skills.
highlights: Follow-on project funding for top participants
content:
Our Youth Leadership Workshop is a transformative three-day experience designed for young leaders aged 18–30 who want to make a tangible difference in their communities. Participants emerge with practical skills in conflict resolution, public speaking, and civic engagement — and a concrete action plan for a peace project in their community.

Since 2023, over 800 young leaders have graduated from our workshops. Alumni have gone on to found community peace initiatives, run for local office, and become trained mediators in their own right. The workshop combines interactive training, mentorship from seasoned peacebuilders, and hands-on project design sessions.

All meals and materials are provided. Travel scholarships are available for participants from outside Kampala. Limited to 150 participants — early application is strongly recommended.

title: Community Health Fair
tagline: Free health care, nutrition counseling, and wellness education
description: Free health screenings, nutrition counseling, and wellness education for underserved communities in the Great Lakes region.
highlights: Free blood pressure, glucose, and HIV screenings
content:
The Community Health Fair is Peace League Africa's flagship health outreach event, bringing free medical services and wellness education to communities that lack access to quality health care. Our 2026 fair in Kigali expects to serve over 2,000 community members with free screenings, immunizations, and health education.

In partnership with the Rwanda Ministry of Health and local health centers, we provide comprehensive health services including blood pressure and diabetes screenings, HIV testing and counseling, child immunizations, nutrition assessments, and mental health first aid. Community health workers are on-site to connect attendees with ongoing care.

No appointment necessary. Walk-ins welcome. All services are completely free. Transportation assistance available for elderly and disabled attendees.

title: Annual Gala & Fundraiser
tagline: A night of celebration, stories, and supporting peace
description: Our flagship fundraising event featuring guest speakers, beneficiary stories, and a celebration of peace-building achievements.
highlights: Live fundraising appeal with matching gift opportunity
content:
Our Annual Gala & Fundraiser is the highlight of Peace League Africa's calendar — a night of inspiration, connection, and impact. Join 350 of our closest supporters, partners, and peacebuilders for an unforgettable evening celebrating a decade of transforming conflict into hope across 12 African countries.

This year's gala features moving beneficiary stories from the field, a keynote address from our Executive Director, and a live fundraising appeal with a generous matching gift opportunity. Every dollar raised at the gala will be matched 2x by our board of directors, doubling your impact.

Attire: African formal / Black tie. Your ticket includes a three-course dinner, drinks, and entry to our silent auction featuring African art, safari packages, and exclusive experiences. Tables of 10 are available for organizations.

title: Water Well Inauguration — Turkana
description: Join us as we commission our 50th clean water well, providing safe drinking water to over 5,000 community members.
highlights: Community celebration with music and dance
Tree planting around the well site
content:
We invite you to join us in celebrating a milestone: the commissioning of our 50th clean water well in Turkana County, providing safe drinking water to over 5,000 community members. Each well represents countless hours of community collaboration, geological surveys, and sustainable engineering.

Our water program has transformed lives across Kenya's arid regions. Before our wells, women and girls would walk up to 6 hours daily to collect water from contaminated sources. Now, they have clean water within 15 minutes of their homes — giving girls time to attend school and women time to pursue livelihoods.

The celebration will feature traditional music and dance, speeches from community leaders, and a ceremonial well-pumping. Light refreshments will be provided. All are welcome.

title: Interfaith Dialogue Forum
description: Religious and community leaders gather to foster mutual understanding and build bridges across faith traditions.
highlights: Opening prayer circle with all faiths represented
content:
The Interfaith Dialogue Forum brings together religious leaders, scholars, and community representatives from Christian, Muslim, traditional African, and other faith traditions for a day of mutual understanding and collaborative action. Hosted at the African Union Headquarters, the forum symbolizes our shared commitment to peace across all divides.

In regions where religious difference has been exploited to divide communities, the forum creates space for honest conversation, shared learning, and joint action. Participants explore peace teachings across traditions, share strategies for preventing faith-based violence, and develop interfaith community projects.

All dietary needs are accommodated. Translation services available in Amharic, Arabic, English, French, and Swahili.

title: Peace Run 2026
tagline: Running together for peace across Nairobi
description: Hundreds of runners came together to promote peace and raise funds for community programs across the city.
content:
The Peace Run 2026 brought together 1,800 runners from across Nairobi and beyond for a day of unity, fitness, and fundraising. Participants ran 5K or 10K through the streets of Nairobi, ending at Uhuru Gardens for a celebration featuring live music, food vendors, and community booths.

Funds raised support Peace League Africa's youth mentorship programs and peace education initiatives in Nairobi-area schools. Thank you to everyone who participated, volunteered, and donated. See you at Peace Run 2027!

If you missed the run but still want to support, donations are accepted year-round at our donate page.

title: Women in Peacebuilding Conference
tagline: Celebrating the role of women in conflict resolution
description: A landmark conference celebrating the role of women in conflict resolution and community reconciliation.
highlights: Keynote by Nobel Peace Prize nominee
Launch of Women Peacebuilders Network Uganda chapter
content:
Our Women in Peacebuilding Conference was a landmark gathering of 280 women peacebuilders from across Uganda and the Great Lakes region. The conference celebrated the critical — and often unrecognized — role women play in conflict resolution, community reconciliation, and peacebuilding at every level.

Highlights included a powerful keynote address, hands-on workshops in trauma-informed mediation and community organizing, and the official launch of the Uganda chapter of our Women Peacebuilders Network. The energy in the room was palpable — a reminder that when women lead, peace follows.

Conference proceedings and recordings are available on request. Contact us for more information.

title: Clean Water Awareness Day
tagline: Celebrating water conservation and new wells
description: Community members gathered to learn about water conservation and celebrate the completion of three new wells.
highlights: Tree planting along the lake shore
content:
Clean Water Awareness Day brought together 650 community members at the Kisumu Waterfront for a day of learning, celebration, and action. The event marked the completion of three new clean water wells serving communities in Kisumu County, providing safe drinking water to over 3,500 people.

Attendees learned about water conservation techniques, well maintenance, and hygiene best practices through interactive booths and demonstrations. Children enjoyed educational games and activities. The day concluded with a tree planting session along the lake shore, symbolizing our commitment to environmental stewardship alongside water access.

Thank you to the Kisumu County Government and local community leaders for their partnership in making this event a success.
